mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical to defensive demand, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60d returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample windows, or partial correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 65 percent of triggered tightening episodes, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate hiring, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between PAYEMS growth and 5d SPX returns falls below 0.10, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annualized, economic momentum accelerates profit margins and equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit and stagnant industrial production indicates debt-fueled consumption masking underlying supply weakness, increasing earnings recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60d returns in more than 50 percent of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid wage growth alongside rising unemployment, structural labor market deterioration triggers earnings downgrades and equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of triggered contraction episodes, or the joint signal predictive accuracy does not exceed 55 percent in walk-forward testing.
